title: Madhavi is an artist and household authority not a repentant stereotype
source_observation: Madhavi enters the story with extensive artistic history public recognition motherhood and grief
risk: The film could reduce her to a seductive past followed by moral purification
decision: Present artistic intelligence household authority grief and religious conviction together and reject the fallen-woman redemption template
rationale: Preserves the character's knowledge dignity and contradiction
required_review: Classical Tamil; performance history; gender studies; actor preparation

title: Renunciation reorganises life rather than purifying an impure woman
source_observation: Madhavi withdraws from public performance after catastrophe but her body knowledge household and obligations remain
risk: A jewellery-removal montage could imply that art sexuality or womanhood caused moral pollution
decision: Show changes to time work patronage dress resources and relationships while retaining artistic history and embodied dignity
rationale: Makes renunciation a demanding social vocation instead of misogynistic shorthand
required_review: Buddhist studies; classical Tamil; gender history; costume; production design

title: Maternal protection does not grant ownership of Manimekalai's vocation
source_observation: Madhavi seeks to protect her daughter from an inherited public and erotic future
risk: The mother's fear could be treated as sufficient authority to assign renunciation to the daughter
decision: Let Madhavi advise protect and set boundaries while requiring her to recognise Manimekalai as an independent moral agent
rationale: Preserves maternal love without reproducing coercion
required_review: Screenplay; gender studies; family-dynamics consultant; actor preparation

title: Madhavi's trained body remains visible after performance ends
source_observation: Madhavi's artistic formation precedes renunciation and cannot be physically forgotten
risk: Renunciation could be acted through weakness bodily collapse or complete loss of rhythm
decision: Retain breath balance timing gaze and spatial intelligence without importing modern Bharatanatyam choreography
rationale: Makes biography visible while respecting period uncertainty
required_review: Performance history; movement coach; classical Tamil; choreography

title: Kannagi and Madhavi are not arranged as pure wife versus fallen performer
source_observation: Both women are connected to Kovalan and to the Madurai catastrophe through different histories
risk: Cross-epic compression could create a moral contest based on marital or sexual status
decision: Represent distinct suffering choices and responsibilities without using one woman to condemn the other
rationale: Prevents a reductive patriarchal hierarchy from replacing the epics' complexity
required_review: Silappathikaram specialist; gender studies; screenplay; publicity

title: Grief changes routines decisions and authority
source_observation: Madhavi's withdrawal follows catastrophic loss and affects her public and household life
risk: Grief could appear only as repeated crying or decorative silence
decision: Track grief through room use schedules access speech work and maternal control as well as moments of open sorrow
rationale: Gives the actor playable continuity and connects emotion to material consequences
required_review: Trauma-informed consultant; actor preparation; production design; continuity

title: The performance household's workers and dependants remain visible
source_observation: Public art depends on teachers musicians flower workers costume labour servants and household resources
risk: Renunciation could be framed as an individual spiritual choice with no effect on others
decision: Show who loses work changes roles preserves knowledge or remains dependent when performance stops
rationale: Connects spiritual choice with labour and household responsibility
required_review: Labour history; performance history; production design; screenplay

title: Madhavi and Chitrapathi receive competing intelligent survival logics
source_observation: Madhavi seeks withdrawal while Chitrapathi seeks continuity of lineage income and public standing
risk: The older woman could become a greedy comic villain and Madhavi an unquestioned moral authority
decision: Give both women specific material reasoning shared professional language and accountability for coercive choices
rationale: Creates credible intergenerational conflict rather than moral caricature
required_review: Classical Tamil; performance history; gender studies; actor preparation

title: Artistic objects are not destroyed as renunciation shorthand
source_observation: The evidence does not establish a dramatic destruction of instruments costumes or jewellery
risk: A familiar renunciation montage could manufacture a symbolic act and imply hatred of art
decision: Use storage redistribution gifting altered use or documented disuse only where supported and keep uncertainty visible
rationale: Protects textual integrity and avoids equating spirituality with cultural destruction
required_review: Classical Tamil; props; costume; production design; screenplay

title: Madhavi's mature arc ends in release without maternal disappearance
source_observation: Manimekalai develops an independent vocation while Madhavi remains her mother and part of the household world
risk: The film could either retain controlling motherhood or erase Madhavi once the heroine becomes autonomous
decision: Give Madhavi a final act of listening acknowledgement or household reorganisation that releases control without abandoning relationship
rationale: Completes her internal arc while avoiding unsupported dominance over the ending
required_review: Original-text review; screenplay; director; actor preparation
